page: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 3 physical cores and 6 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX24-6ms_v2 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core (2237.0) and single-threaded gzip compression (34.87 GB/sec). Single-threaded text processing also ranks in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core (6658.27) and Geekbench multi-core (6780.0), falls within the average range. Memory performance shows a stark contrast: small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 518.22 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached read bandwidth (35.26 GB/sec) and the overall PassMark memory score are positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given its massive memory capacity of 504.0 GB relative to only 6 vCPUs, this instance is highly specialized for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel compute scaling. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-heavy data processing tasks. The inclusion of AMX instructions provides hardware acceleration for low-parameter LLM inference, though text generation for medium and large models remains a bottleneck. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a high resource density for memory, making it a specialized choice where RAM capacity is the primary constraint rather than raw multi-core compute power.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Configured with 3 physical cores and 6 vCPUs, the instance features a massive 504.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 84.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2237.0 and high single-threaded gzip compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and large-block uncached memory bandwidth remain average to weak. This extreme memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the FX24-6ms_v2 highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, such as large in-memory databases, caching systems, and specialized data processing workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor and 504.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads and in-memory database applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 vCPUs, 504.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 4.2 GHz.
tagline: A highly specialized memory-optimized instance featuring an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Massive 504.0 GB of RAM providing 84.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks
No local storage or GPU resources included
Optimized for large in-memory databases and caching layers
